papers.title_en: A Unified Maximum-Likelihood Framework for 3D InISAR Phase Unwrapping with Outlier Rejection
papers.authors: Matteo Pardi, Francesco Mancuso, Elisa Giusti, Marco Martorella
papers.evidence: 适用于InSAR智能处理的InISAR相位解缠方法
papers.tldr: 三维干涉ISAR相位解缠面临歧义和异常值挑战。本文提出基于混合整数最小二乘的最大似然框架，逐散射体解缠，无需空间连续性，可统一处理多基线、多频率或混合设置。该框架自然产生后验质量指标用于统计异常值检测。蒙特卡罗仿真验证了在L型双频设置下的可靠3D重建，计算成本适于实际系统。
papers.abstract_en: This paper presents a novel mathematical framework for phase unwrapping in three-dimensional interferometric ISAR (3D InISAR) imaging. The approach works on a scatterer-by-scatterer basis and does not rely on any spatial continuity assumptions, making it suitable for sparse point clouds. The formulation is derived from the Mixed-Integer Least Squares (MILS) theory, an optimal maximum-likelihood framework for joint estimation of integer and real unknowns in the presence of Gaussian noise. This provides a unified way to handle generic sensor geometries, multi-baseline, multi-frequency, or hybrid setups. The method also produces a natural a posteriori quality metric for each unwrapped phase, which can be used to build a statistical test to reject outliers. The algorithm is simple to implement and has a computational cost suitable for operational systems. This paper presents the theoretical foundations of the framework and a first validation study on a standard L-shaped dual-frequency setup using Monte Carlo simulations. Results show that the proposed framework enables reliable 3D reconstruction in challenging ambiguity conditions.
